title: Test isolation: row17 fallback must not log into the real repo
action: runHookRaw spawns the hook without a cwd option, so row17's process.cwd() fallback resolves to whatever directory the suite runs from — in this repo that appended real lines to .bee/logs/dispatch.jsonl. Give runHookRaw an optional spawnCwd param (spawnSync cwd option), pass enabledRoot for row17 (fallback then evaluates against the fixture and logs there — same semantics, isolated) and noRepoRoot for the junk/null/array rows for consistency. Assert row17's dispatch line lands in the fixture log.

truths: Running the guard suite from the repo root leaves the repo's .bee/logs/dispatch.jsonl untouched

artifacts.substantive: runHookRaw takes spawnCwd; row17 asserts fixture-side logging

prohibitions: No weakening of row17's fallback assertion

verify: L1=$(wc -l < .bee/logs/dispatch.jsonl); node hooks/test_model_guard.mjs > /dev/null; L2=$(wc -l < .bee/logs/dispatch.jsonl); [ "$L1" = "$L2" ] && echo NO-POLLUTION && node hooks/test_model_guard.mjs | tail -1

trace.outcome: runHookRaw takes spawnCwd; row17 pinned to enabledRoot with a fixture-side log assertion, junk/null/array rows pinned to noRepoRoot. RED: 1 polluted line per suite run (before=5 after=6). GREEN: NO-POLLUTION, ALL PASS.
trace.verify_command: line-count .bee/logs/dispatch.jsonl unchanged across a suite run && suite tail
